summary: Backend-only regression + feature verification for Google reCAPTCHA v3 protection on portal Login/Register/Forgot-Password. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_recaptcha_auth.py (13 tests) covering public /auth/config shape, admin schema, PUT persistence + secret masking, fail-open when disabled, enforcement when enabled (missing/garbage token), site_key exposure toggle, disable-again immediacy, and legacy-client backward compatibility. All 13 new tests pass. Existing test_assets_straight_line.py (14) and test_finance_v2.py (10) still pass — no regression. Integration state left disabled after the run.
critical_code_review_comments: routes.py is ~4530 lines — pre-existing concern; the two recaptcha guards + /auth/config additions are clean but the file itself should be split per-domain.

test_portal.py hard-codes an old preview URL fallback ('cloud-services-id.preview.emergentagent.com') for REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — the test file 404s in isolation unless PORTAL_API_BASE=localhost is exported. Not a recaptcha regression; passes when correct env is present. Recommend making the fallback localhost:8001 for consistency with test_recaptcha_auth.py and test_assets_straight_line.py.

RecaptchaV3Verifier.verify raises HTTPException(status_code=500) when secret_key is empty but enabled=true. Better UX would be to treat missing secret as disabled+log-warning (fail-open) or reject at PUT time to prevent an admin accidentally locking themselves out. Not blocking for this iteration.

get_recaptcha_settings returns the raw doc (with the secret_key). /auth/config only reads credentials.site_key, so no leak — but the helper's name is easy to misuse; consider a `get_recaptcha_public_config()` helper that returns only {enabled, site_key} for public endpoints.

success_rate.frontend: not tested (backend-only request; frontend helper /app/frontend/src/portal/recaptcha.js reviewed statically — correctly returns null when server reports enabled=false, verified indirectly via backend fail-open tests)

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: register creates a TEST_recap_reg_<uuid>@example.com user which is deleted immediately via /admin/users/{id} after the test. No lasting DB state. The integration_settings.recaptcha document is wiped at class setup and set to enabled:false at teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: reCAPTCHA integration = provider 'recaptcha' in integration_settings collection. Backend module /app/backend/portal/integrations_v2.py (INTEGRATION_SCHEMA at ~L564, RecaptchaV3Verifier at ~L735, get_recaptcha_settings/enforce_recaptcha at ~L829-845). Route guards in /app/backend/portal/routes.py at ~L96 (/auth/config public), ~L122 (login), ~L180 (register), ~L3300 (forgot-password). Models LoginIn/RegisterIn/ForgotPasswordIn all have optional recaptcha_token: Optional[str] = None — backward-compat preserved. Tests are grouped inside a SINGLE class TestRecaptchaAuth so pytest-xdist's default loadscope pins them to one worker (they mutate a shared integration_settings doc and MUST run sequentially). If splitting into multiple classes, use pytestmark = pytest.mark.xdist_group('recaptcha_auth') plus --dist=loadgroup. Fake site_key='6LcFakeSiteKey...' / secret='6LcSecretKey...' are used to exercise Google's real siteverify (returns success=false with invalid-input-secret which yields HTTP 400/403 from our route — exactly what test_09 asserts). Integration state is DISABLED at end of test class teardown; re-run safe.
verified_scenarios: GET /auth/config disabled default → {recaptcha:{enabled:false,site_key:null}}, no 'secret' string anywhere in response
GET /admin/integrations-v2/schema → recaptcha entry with category='security', credentials={site_key,secret_key}, options⊇{min_score,expected_hostname,verify_action}

PUT /admin/integrations-v2/recaptcha with fake keys → returns redacted (site_key='', site_key_masked='6LcF*******', secret_key='', secret_key_masked='6LcS*******'). Subsequent GET /admin/integrations-v2 also redacted the same way. Raw secret/site key never appear in response text.
Recaptcha OFF: login/register/forgot without recaptcha_token → 200/200/200 (or 202). No 'recaptcha' in forgot response body.
Recaptcha ON, no token: login → 400 with body containing 'recaptcha' + 'missing'. Same for register + forgot-password.

Recaptcha ON, garbage token 'deadbeef'*8: login → 400 (Google returns success=false with invalid-input-response/invalid-input-secret), body does NOT contain 'invalid email or password' → confirms recaptcha guard runs BEFORE password check.
Recaptcha ON: GET /auth/config returns {enabled:true, site_key:'6LcFake...'} but NO secret_key field, and FAKE_SECRET_KEY string is not in response body.
Disable via PUT enabled:false → /auth/config immediately shows enabled:false + site_key:null; login without token works again.
Legacy client payload {email,password} without recaptcha_token field → 200 when disabled (backward-compat).
